task_title: Reformat slide title and body text in LibreOffice Impress
instruction: Bold the entire body paragraph and exit text editing.
task_goal: The body paragraph is formatted in bold while preserving its existing italic style, and editing mode is exited.

steps.step_goal: Activate body text editing Select the entire body paragraph
action_preconditions: The enlarged underlined title is visible, and the italic bullet paragraph below it is not selected.
The body text box is active and the word "author's" is selected.

nl_position: the word "author's" in the bullet paragraph near the middle of the slide

steps.action_before_state: The screenshot shows the LibreOffice Impress application open with a presentation titled "69_4.pptx". The current slide, Slide 1 of 1, is displayed in Normal view. The slide has a title, “The History of Art”, formatted in bold, underlined, and highlighted with a light peach background. Below the title is a bulleted paragraph defining art as a diverse range of human activities creating visual, auditory, or performing artifacts for aesthetic or emotional appreciation. The text is in Times New Roman, size 50 pt. On the right, the Properties panel is open showing Character and Paragraph formatting options. The left sidebar displays the slide thumbnails, with the current slide selected. The top menu bar includes File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, Slide Show, Tools, Window, and Help. The system clock in the top bar indicates March 25 at 17:53. The interface is running on a Linux desktop environment.
action_after_effects: The entire bullet paragraph becomes highlighted for formatting.
steps.nl_explanation: Double-click the word "author's" in the bullet paragraph to enter body-text editing and select that word. Click the selected word again to expand the selection to the whole paragraph.

action_preconditions: The full bullet paragraph is selected in the body text box.
steps.action_before_state: The screenshot shows LibreOffice Impress open with a presentation titled "69_4.pptx". The current slide, labeled "Slide 1 of 1", is titled "The History of Art" in bold, underlined text. Below the title is a paragraph of text defining art, which is currently selected and highlighted in light orange. The text reads: “Art is a diverse range of human activities in creating visual, auditory or performing artifacts, expressing the author’s imaginative, conceptual ideas, or technical skill, intended to be appreciated primarily for their beauty or emotional power.” The Properties panel on the right is open to the “Character” and “Paragraph” sections, showing font settings (Arial, 32 pt) and spacing/indent values. The application menu bar at the top includes File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, Slide Show, Tools, Window, Help. The left sidebar displays slide thumbnails, with the current slide visible. The system clock in the top bar indicates “Mar 25 17:54”. The Ubuntu desktop environment is visible with docked application icons on the left.
action_after_effects: Bold formatting is applied to the selected paragraph while it remains italic and selected.
steps.nl_explanation: Press Ctrl+B to make the selected paragraph bold.

action_preconditions: The bold italic body paragraph is still selected in text-edit mode.

nl_position: a blank white area on the slide to the left of the body text box

steps.action_before_state:
The screenshot shows the LibreOffice Impress application open with a presentation titled "69_4.pptx". The current slide, labeled as Slide 1 of 1, is titled "The History of Art" in bold, underlined text. Below the title, there is a paragraph of text defining art as a diverse range of human activities involving visual, auditory, or performing artifacts that express imaginative, conceptual, or technical ideas and are appreciated for their beauty or emotional power. The paragraph is highlighted with a light orange background and appears to be selected for editing.

On the left, the Slides pane displays a thumbnail of the current slide. The top menu bar includes standard options like File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, Slide Show, Tools, Window, and Help. The toolbar below contains various formatting and editing icons.

On the right side, the Properties panel is open, showing tabs for Character, Lists, and Paragraph. The Character tab is active, displaying font settings (Arial, 32 pt), and the Paragraph tab shows spacing and indent values. The cursor is positioned within the text box, indicated by a red crosshair.

The system clock at the top indicates the date and time as “Mar 25 17:54”. The bottom status bar shows slide information, dimensions, and language setting (“English (USA)”). The overall interface suggests the user is editing or formatting the content of the slide.
action_after_effects: The text selection is cleared and the slide returns to normal object view with the new formatting visible.
steps.nl_explanation: Click empty slide space to clear the text selection and leave edit mode.
